title: Yellow Jacket Removal in West Farms, Bronx | $350-$550
metaDescription: Professional yellow jacket removal in West Farms, Bronx. Safe, effective nest elimination for homes and businesses. Licensed exterminators. Call now for fast service!
intro: Yellow jackets pose a serious threat to West Farms residents and businesses, particularly during late summer and early fall when colonies are at their peak. Our professional yellow jacket removal service safely eliminates nests from homes, apartment buildings, and commercial properties throughout the West Farms neighborhood. With same-day and emergency service available, we protect your family and customers from painful stings and allergic reactions.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served West Farms for years with licensed, experienced technicians who understand the unique challenges of yellow jacket infestations in Bronx neighborhoods. We use EPA-approved treatments and provide guaranteed results, with pricing between $350-$550 depending on nest location and size.
localContext: West Farms' mix of residential buildings, the Bronx Zoo proximity, and green spaces near the Bronx River create ideal conditions for yellow jacket nesting. Our team is familiar with the area's apartment complexes along Boston Road and single-family homes near West Farms Square, providing fast response times throughout the 10460 zip code.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a yellow jacket nest in West Farms?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day service for most West Farms locations and emergency removal when needed. Our technicians typically arrive within 2-4 hours of your call and can complete most yellow jacket nest removals in 45-90 minutes, depending on nest size and accessibility.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 yellow jacket removal price?
faqs.answer: Our pricing includes thorough inspection, complete nest treatment and removal, elimination of all yellow jackets, preventative treatment to discourage re-nesting, and a 30-day service guarantee. The final cost depends on nest location (ground vs. aerial), size, and accessibility.

faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my West Farms family or pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are aggressive when defending their nest and can sting multiple times. Unlike bees, they don't lose their stinger and can attack repeatedly. They pose particular risks to children, pets, and anyone with allergies, making professional removal essential for safety.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have yellow jackets versus other wasps or bees?
faqs.answer: Yellow jackets are smaller than hornets, with bright yellow and black striped patterns and minimal hair. They often nest in ground cavities, wall voids, or eaves, and you'll notice increased activity around the nest entrance. Our technicians provide free identification during inspection to confirm the species before treatment.
